# How to Build a V-Taper Physique: Workouts, Muscles & Training Tips

Monday, 28 July 2025

[How to Build Muscle](https://www.puregym.com/blog/how-to-build-muscle/) [Workout Exercise & Routines](https://www.puregym.com/blog/workout-exercise-routines/) [Fitness Tips & Advice](https://www.puregym.com/blog/fitness-tips-advice/)

![How to Build a V-Taper Physique](https://www.puregym.com/media/slzpjtsp/how-to-build-a-v-taper-physique_blogpost-notitle.jpg?quality=80)

[What Is A V Taper](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#what) \| [How To Get V Taper](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#how) \| [Muscles](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#muscles) \| [Fat Loss](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#fat-loss) \| [Gym Exercises](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#exercises) \| [Calisthenics Exercises](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#calisthenics) \| [Workout Split](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#workout-split) \| [Full Body Workout](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#workout) \| [Nutrition](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#nutrition) \| [Common Mistakes](https://www.puregym.com/blog/v-taper-physique/#mistakes)

Exercise has hundreds of physical and mental health benefits, and one of these benefits is the ability to help change your body composition and physique.

For some, this might be as simple as losing a few pounds or creating an overall more toned appearance. For others, this could be something more specific, such as building a V taper physique. If this is your goal, read on to find out how to achieve the V taper body.

## What Is A V Taper Body?

The V tape physique is a body shape that has wide shoulders and upper back that tapers down to a narrow waist, creating a V silhouette.

The V taper is a widely coveted physique among body builders and fitness enthusiasts alike as, unlike washboard abs or bulging biceps, having a V taper back can be easily spotted even when fully clothed and shows that the person is committed to training.

A V taper body also signifies a balanced training approach as the shoulders and back are clearly worked, vs the more obvious muscles you see in the mirror such as the chest and biceps.

## How To Build A V Taper Body

There are two elements when it comes to getting a V taper physique:

- **Increase the width of your upper back and shoulders**. This step involves [gaining muscle](https://www.puregym.com/blog/the-best-gym-workout-plan-for-gaining-muscle/) in the upper body muscles through dedicated strength training.

- **Reduce the size of your waist.** This is achieved through [losing fat](https://www.puregym.com/blog/how-to-lose-weight/). While it isn't possible to spot reduce from the waist alone, reducing your overall body fat percentage can help to decrease the size of the waist.


As the upper back and shoulder width increases and the waist decreases, the difference in size helps to create the V taper.

## Muscles For V Taper Physique

The key muscles you want to build to create the broad shoulders and upper back needed for a V taper body are:

- [**Latissimus dorsi (lats)**](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/lat-exercises/) \- large muscles running from the lower and muddle back, around the side, to the upper arm

- [**Deltoids**](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/) \- group of muscles that cover the front (anterior delts), back (posterior delts), and sides (lateral delts) of the shoulders

- **Trapezius (traps)** \- trapezoid shaped muscle covering the neck and upper mid back

- **Rhomboids** \- a pair of muscles (rhomboid major and rhomboid minor) in the upper back

- **Obliques** \- pair of muscles that sit on the sides of the abdomen, running from the ribcage to the pelvis


## Fat Loss For A V Taper

Achieving a V taper physique involves building muscle mass while losing fat.

To increase muscle mass, you need to focus on [muscular hypertrophy focused strength training](https://www.puregym.com/blog/hypertrophy-vs-strength/). This involves a high training volume (at least 2-3 times a week per muscle group), with moderate weights, sets and reps. This provides the muscles with the right kind of stimulus in the right amount to encourage efficient muscle growth.

A calorie surplus is also recommended when it comes to building muscle, however this contrasts with the fat loss many people need to achieve a classic V taper. Likewise, a large calorie deficit needed for fat loss can slow down or prevent muscle being built.

There are two ways to tackle this:

- **Body recomposition:** Eating at, or just below, maintenance calories while training to build muscle can help with body recomposition by simultaneously losing fat at the same time as building muscle. In this approach, the energy used to build muscle comes from breaking down body fat. While this approach isn't the most effective way to build muscle or lose fat, it's useful for people who struggle with eating under or over their maintenance calories.

- [**Bulking and cutting**](https://www.puregym.com/blog/a-beginners-guide-to-bulking-and-cutting/) **:** The bulk and cut cycle involves focusing on building muscle and losing fat separately. During the bulking phase, you'll eat in a calorie surplus to encourage muscle growth, and during the cutting phase you'll eat in a calorie deficit for fat loss while maintaining muscle mass through training. This is an efficient way to change your physique but can be mentally challenging, particularly during the cutting phase.


## Best Gym Exercises For V Taper Body

To get a V taper, you want to choose exercises that create enough stimulus for the back and shoulder muscles to grow. Including a mixture of compound exercises, which increases the overall load the muscles are exposed to, and isolation exercises, which allow you to specifically focus on individual muscles, is the best approach.

![Chart explaining the best exercises to achieve a V taper physique, organised into back, shoulder, and core exercises](https://www.puregym.com/media/xezfpiux/best-exercises-for-a-v-taper-physique.jpg?quality=80)

### Back exercises

Target the traps, rhomboids, and lats with these exercises.

**Compound exercises**

- [Pull ups](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/pull-up/)
- [Lat pull downs](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/lat-exercises/)
- [Seated rows](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/rows/seated-cable-row/)
- [Bent over rows](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/rows/barbell-bent-over-row/)

**Isolation exercises**

- [Y raises](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/rear-delt-exercises/y-raises/)

- [Straight arm pushdowns](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/lat-exercises/straight-arm-lat-pulldown/)

- [Face pulls](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/rear-delt-exercises/face-pulls/)

- [Shrugs](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/shrugs/)


### Shoulder exercises

Work all three deltoid muscle groups with these exercises.

**Compound exercises**

- [Overhead press](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/shoulder-press/)

- [Upright rows](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/upright-rows/)

- [Incline chest press](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/chest/bench-press/barbell-incline-bench-press/)

- [Chest dips](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/chest/dips/)


**Isolation exercises**

- [Front raises](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/front-raises/)

- [Lateral raises](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/lateral-raises/)

- [Rear delt flys](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/arms-and-shoulders/rear-delt-exercises/rear-delt-flyes/)


### Core exercises

- [Russian twists](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/abs/russian-twists/)

- [Hanging leg raises](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/abs/hanging-leg-raises/)

- [V sit ups](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/abs/v-sit-ups/)

- [Pallof press](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/abs/pallof-press/)

- [Deadbugs](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/abs/deadbugs/)


## Calisthenics V Taper Exercises

Weightlifting is highly effective when it comes to muscle hypertrophy, but it's not the only way to build a wider back and shoulders. Bodyweight calisthenics exercises can be just as good as long as you are following progressive overload by increasing the volume or intensity over time.

Some good calisthenics exercises for getting a V taper physique include:

**Beginner**

- [Negative or assisted pull ups](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/pull-up/pull-ups/)

- [Pike push ups](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/chest/press-up/pike-push-ups/)

- [Push ups or modified push ups](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/chest/press-up/)

- [Inverted rows](https://www.puregym.com/exercises/back/rows/inverted-row/)


**Intermediate**

- Weighted pull ups

- Muscle ups

- Back lever

- Decline push ups

- Handstand push ups


## Best Workout Split For V Taper

When it comes to significantly changing your physique through exercise, having a solid workout plan is key. Your workout split needs to ensure that each muscle group is hit at least twice a week, without neglecting other muscle groups (to prevent imbalances from occurring). Your workout split should also ensure that each muscle group gets at least a full day’s rest between training sessions, and account for one full rest day each week.
Although you can factor core specific workout days, adding a few core and oblique exercises to each workout is a more effective approach.
There are a few workout splits that work well for getting a V taper:

### 3 Day Workout Split

If you can only commit to working out three days a week, it's best to focus on full body workouts that prioritise compound exercises as this allows you to expose each muscle group to as much load as possible across the week.

**Option 1**

- Monday: Full body

- Wednesday: Full body

- Friday: Full body


**Option 2**

- Monday: Upper body

- Wednesday: Full body

- Friday: Lower body


### 4 Day Workout Split

If you are training four times a week, you can continue to focus on full body workouts or begin to narrow this down. An upper/ lower workout split is a great option here:

- Monday: Upper body

- Tuesday: Lower body

- Thursday: Upper body

- Friday: Lower body


### 5 Day Workout Split

When you begin training five or more days each week, you can narrow your workouts down even further to focus on specific muscle groups. This allows you to increase the volume these muscles are exposed to, furthering hypertrophy.

**Example workout split**

- Monday: Shoulders and chest

- Tuesday: Legs and glutes

- Wednesday: Back and core

- Friday: Legs and glutes

- Saturday: Upper body


### 6 Day Workout Split

There are multiple ways you can organise your workouts with a 6 day workout split to get your V taper. Here are a few examples.

**Option 1: Push pull legs split**

- Monday: Upper body - push

- Tuesday: Legs and glutes

- Wednesday: Upper body - pull

- Thursday: Upper body - push

- Friday: Legs and glutes

- Saturday: Upper body - pull


**Option 2**

- Monday: Shoulders and chest

- Tuesday: Legs and glutes

- Wednesday: Back

- Thursday: Legs

- Friday: Upper body

- Saturday: Legs


## Example Full Body V Taper Gym Workout

Here's a balanced full body workout you can try that targets all the muscles needed to get a V taper, as well as the chest, legs, and glutes.

**Warm up**

- Cross trainer 6/10 effort - 5 minutes

- Arm swings - 30 seconds each side

- Leg swings - 30 seconds each side

- Plank - 30 second hold


**Workout**

1. Superset

- (a) Lat pull downs - 3 x 8-10

- (b) Russian twists - 3 x 12


2. Squat to overhead press - 3 x 8-10

3. Superset


- (a) Bent over row - 3 x 8-10

- (b) Hanging leg lift - 3 x 10


4. Incline chest press - 3 - 8-10

5. Deadlifts - 4 x 6-8


**Cooldown**

- Overhead tricep stretch - 30 seconds each side

- Standing chest stretch - 30 seconds each side

- Puppy pose - 30 seconds

- Standing quad stretch - 30 seconds each side

- Pigeon pose - 30 seconds each side


## How To Achieve V Taper: Nutrition

Paying attention to your nutrition is important if you want to get a V taper. Without the right diet, it will be difficult to achieve the balance of [fat loss](https://www.puregym.com/blog/how-to-lose-weight/) and [muscle gain](https://www.puregym.com/blog/how-to-build-muscle/) needed for this body composition.

Many people will need to lose some fat to build a V taper physique as it requires low body fat to achieve the broad back to small waist V taper, however cutting your calories too low will make it much harder to build muscle. Eating at a small calorie deficit of around 200 calories will help with steady weight loss while still allowing for muscle gain.

### What to eat

- **Protein:** eating plenty of protein (at least [1.4g protein per kg of bodyweight](https://www.puregym.com/blog/how-much-protein-should-i-eat/)) across the day will help to keep you full while giving your body the amino acids it needs to build muscle. Aim for around 30g protein for each meal rather than eating it all in one go, choosing foods like chicken, eggs, fish, and dairy where possible and supplementing with protein shakes where necessary.

- **Carbs:** carbohydrates provide your body with the fuel needed for intense workouts. Prioritise complex, fibre rich carbs like wholewheat pasta, sweet potatoes, and legumes.

- **Fats:** dietary fats are important for overall health and hormones, as well as satiety (and taste!). Include small amounts of [healthy fats](https://www.puregym.com/blog/good-fats-and-bad-fats-explained/) in your meals rather than avoid this food group.

- **Micronutrients:** aim to eat a variety of fruit and veg each day to ensure you are getting the nutrients and minerals needed for optimal health, as well as enough dietary fibre each day.


## Common Mistakes That Kill Your V Taper Progress

Before you begin working on your V taper physique, here are some common mistakes to avoid.

### Not progressive overloading

To build significant muscle and strength, you need to progressive overload your workouts. This means increasing the intensity or volume of your workouts each week by increasing the weights, reps, or workout duration, rather than doing the same amount each week.

Learn more about [progressive overload here](https://www.puregym.com/blog/progressive-overload/).

### Not following a workout plan

Changing up your workouts every week makes it harder to be consistent and to increase the intensity over time, making it much harder to see results. Stick to the same exercises and workouts for at least 8-12 weeks, focusing on progressive overload. After 8-12 weeks you can adjust or switch up your workouts if preferred.

### Not training hard enough

Building muscle is hard and requires the body to be sufficiently challenged. You should aim to train close to failure in your workouts, which means stopping when you’re just 1-2 reps away from being unable to lift another rep.

### Overtraining obliques

Training obliques is important in building a V taper physique, but you should avoid building too much muscle mass here as it can thicken the waist, making that V shape less pronounced.

Ready to get a V taper physique? Get more [workout ideas here](https://www.puregym.com/blog/workout-exercise-routines/) or find your [nearest gym here](https://www.puregym.com/gyms/) to get started.

# How to Get the V Taper Physique With Calisthenics

![How to Get the V Taper Physique With Calisthenics](https://gravity.fitness/cdn/shop/articles/Get-The_-VTaper-Physique.png?v=1772796967&width=2048)

The V taper physique is one of the most sought after body shapes in fitness. Broad shoulders, a wide back, and a narrow waist create the classic athletic silhouette seen in gymnasts, fighters, and elite calisthenics athletes.

Unlike bodybuilding approaches that rely heavily on machines and isolation exercises, calisthenics builds this shape naturally through compound bodyweight movements. With the right exercises and a consistent routine, you can develop impressive shoulder width, powerful lats, and a tight core using minimal equipment.

In this guide, we explain how to build the V taper using calisthenics, the key muscles involved, and the best exercises to include in your training.

## What Is the V Taper Physique?

The V taper refers to the visual ratio between your upper body and waist. The goal is to create the illusion of a triangular upper body where the shoulders and back are significantly wider than the midsection.

This look is created through the development of several key muscle groups:

**Latissimus dorsi (lats)** \- These large muscles on the back are the primary driver of upper body width.

**Side deltoids -** The side delts create the rounded shoulder cap that makes the upper body appear broader.

**Upper chest** \- A well developed upper chest adds thickness to the torso and enhances the overall silhouette.

**Core and waist control**\- A strong core stabilises the body and keeps the waist tight rather than bulky.

Many training systems overcomplicate the process, but the truth is that a relatively small number of effective movements can build this shape. The core idea is simple: focus on strong pulling movements for back width, overhead pressing for shoulder development, and core work that strengthens the midsection without increasing waist size.

[![](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0276/0635/1961/files/GravityCTAv2-Desktop.png?v=1714650528)](https://gravity.fitness/collections/calisthenics-unconventional-fitness-equipment "Calsithenics Store")

## Why Calisthenics Builds a Better V Taper

[Calisthenics](https://gravity.fitness/ "Calsithenics") naturally emphasises the muscles that create the V taper. Pull ups, dips, rows, and push ups recruit multiple muscle groups and require strong stabilisation through the core.

This produces several advantages. First, bodyweight training heavily recruits the lats and shoulders. Pulling movements in particular are excellent for building back width.

Second, calisthenics keeps the core engaged throughout most exercises. This develops strong abdominal muscles without adding unnecessary bulk to the waist.

Third, progressive overload can be achieved using advanced variations or tools such as [weighted vests](https://gravity.fitness/collections/weighted-vests "Weighted Vests"), which allow athletes to continue building muscle once basic movements become easy.

For anyone training at home, this makes calisthenics one of the most efficient ways to build an aesthetic physique.

## The Best Calisthenics Exercises for a V Taper

A strong V taper can be developed using a small number of compound exercises that target the back, shoulders, chest, and core.

### Pull Ups

Pull ups are arguably the most important exercise for building a wide back. They directly target the lats, which are responsible for the outward flare that creates the V shape.

Focus on pulling your elbows down towards your ribs rather than simply lifting your chin over the bar. This cue ensures the lats do most of the work.

A high quality pull up bar is essential for this movement and forms the foundation of most calisthenics programs.

**Inverted Rows** \- Inverted rows develop the mid back, rhomboids, and rear delts. These muscles add thickness to the upper back and improve posture.

Using an underhand grip increases bicep involvement and helps develop the dense upper back often associated with elite calisthenics athletes.

Comprehensive Guide to Achievin…

### Pike Push Ups

Pike push ups are one of the best bodyweight exercises for building strong shoulders. They shift the load toward the deltoids, especially the side and front delts, which contribute heavily to shoulder width.

As strength improves, this movement can progress toward handstand push ups.

**Dips** \- Dips build the chest and triceps while also developing shoulder stability. Leaning slightly forward during the movement increases chest activation and creates the thick upper torso that complements a wide back.

### Decline Push Ups

Elevating the feet during push ups shifts more load to the upper chest and shoulders. This creates the chest shelf effect that enhances the V taper look.

Using parallettes or push up handles can increase the range of motion and improve muscle activation.

### Core Training for a Smaller Waist

A narrow waist is essential for creating contrast with the shoulders and back.

Instead of heavy weighted ab exercises that can thicken the waist, calisthenics often focuses on anti extension movements that strengthen the core without increasing bulk.

Exercises such as dragon flags, hollow body holds, and hanging leg raises develop deep abdominal strength while maintaining a tight midsection.

## Sample V Taper Calisthenics Workout

A simple routine performed three to four times per week is enough to build noticeable progress.

Pull ups - 4 sets

Dips - 4 sets

Pike push ups - 3 sets

Inverted rows - 3 sets

Decline push ups - 3 sets

Hanging leg raises or dragon flags - 3 sets

As strength improves, adding a weighted vest can significantly increase intensity and accelerate muscle growth.

## Nutrition for Building the V Taper

Training builds the stimulus for muscle growth, but nutrition determines how effectively your body can recover and develop. The key nutritional priorities are:

**High protein intake** \- Protein supports muscle repair and growth. Whole food sources such as beef, eggs, yogurt, and fish are excellent options.

**Adequate carbohydrates** \- Carbohydrates fuel performance during demanding calisthenics workouts.

**Healthy fats** \- Dietary fats support hormone production and overall recovery.

Simple meals based on whole foods tend to produce the best results. A high protein breakfast, a protein rich lunch, and a balanced evening meal are often enough to support consistent training progress.

## Equipment That Helps Build the V Taper

One of the biggest advantages of calisthenics is that very little equipment is required. However, a few key tools can dramatically improve your training.

**Pull up bars** \- Essential for developing the lats and upper back.

**Parallettes**\- Allow deeper push ups, dips, and core exercises.

**Weighted vests** \- Add resistance to bodyweight movements and allow continued progression once basic exercises become easy.

These tools make it possible to build a strong and aesthetic physique at home without relying on a traditional gym.

The V taper physique is not created through endless exercise variety or complicated training plans. Instead, it is built by consistently performing powerful compound movements that develop the back, shoulders, and chest while maintaining a strong and tight core.

Pull ups, dips, rows, and push up variations form the foundation of this approach. Combined with simple nutrition and progressive overload using tools such as weighted vests, these movements can produce dramatic results.

With the right consistency and training structure, calisthenics can build one of the most visually impressive physiques in fitness.

And that's why in my opinion, they go hand in hand. And first, we're going to be going over your diet. Now, if you're skinny or skinny fat, that means you need to be lean bulking. And that just means that you need to be eating slightly above your maintenance calories to build muscle without getting fat. And I'm going to be showing you exactly how to lean bulk in just a second. And if you're overweight or fat, the number one tip that I could give you is to cut out the junk food, the sugar, and eat cleaner and focus on hitting your protein goal. It's honestly really simple. Don't over complicate it. Now, when it comes to lean bulking, here's what you need to do. First thing you need to do is to find your maintenance calorie. And that just means the number of calories you need to eat to maintain your current weight. And the easiest way to do this is by going on Google and typing in calorie calculator and clicking on the first link that pops up. And when you get there, just put your information and it's going to calculate your maintenance calories for you. Now, the number you're going to get is obviously just an estimation. So, what I want you to do is to weigh yourself every single day for a full week. And for those days, I want you to eat 200 to 300 calories above that number that you get in the calculator. So, for example, if you get 2500 calories for your maintenance, then I want you to eat 2700 to 2,800 for a full week and weigh yourself every single day. Now, ideally, what you should see is your weight move up after that first week of eating those calories. And if it doesn't happen, then all you got to do is add 200 to 300 calories to the number that you had at the start. So, from 2700 to 2,800, you move up to 2900 to 3,000 calories for another week. And you keep an eye on your weight. And you also want to focus on eating at least one gram of protein per pound of body weight to make sure that you're gaining muscle. So, for example, if you're 150 lbs, then you need to focus on hitting 150 grams of protein every single day. And for the rest of the macros, I always recommend to eat at least 50 grams of healthy fats, and you can fill the rest with your carbs. And don't overthink it, right? Don't stress about perfect timing or the number of meals you need to hit, just eat clean and hit your target of calories and protein, and you're going to be seeing progress in no time. But before you go crazy with the exercise and the diet, just remember the last part that I'm going to be going over, and that is your mindset. Now, here's the truth. Even if your genetics are not great, it's not an excuse. You're never going to know what your true potential is unless you really take it seriously. Most people that I've worked with before think that they have bad genetics, but the truth is they haven't been consistent long enough to really see their potential. And honestly, most of them find it easier to complain than to work on their physique. And always remember this, when you mess up, whether it's a cheat meal, it's a skipped workout, it's a bad day. Don't spiral down. Don't wake up the next day thinking, "Oh, I just messed up." Just wake up and be like, "All right, that cheat meal, it didn't even happen." And then you move forward. Because all of this that you're watching, all of these videos, the goal is to make you feel better, not worse. And honestly, the number one tip that I've received in my journey that helped me the most is this one. What you do next is always going to matter more than what you did last. So, stop overthinking, stop feeling bad for yourself, and just move forward and make sure that the next step you take is the best one you can take. But yeah, now you know exactly how to build a Vtaper physique. And if you want to do it faster with a personalized training split, a custom diet, and a one-on-one coaching with me directly, just DM me the word oneonone on Instagram, and I'll get back to you. Or you can just apply with the first link in this video's description. So, if you want results like my clients here, apply now and I'll help you do it the right way naturally. So, yeah, that's pretty much it for this video. Let me know in the comments which part of this video helped you the most so I can make sure I include more of them in my next videos. Like and subscribe if you enjoyed this video. And yeah, that's pretty much it. Love you all. Peace.

[Bodybuilding](https://generationiron.com/bodybuilding/)

![Frank Zane](https://generationiron.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/05/header-9-1024x543.jpg)

This post may contain affiliate links ( [disclosure policy](https://generationiron.com/disclosure/)).

## A Comprehensive Guide to Developing the Perfect V-Shape

When it comes to bodybuilding and aesthetics, the ultimate goal is to develop a symmetrical, well-rounded physique. While [**modern-day bodybuilding**](https://generationiron.com/mr-olympia-winners/) has moved more towards building pure size with bodybuilders like **[Nick Walker](https://generationiron.com/nick-walker-profile/)** and **[Derek Lunsford](https://generationiron.com/derek-lunsford-profile-stats/)** absolutely killing it in the sport, athletes in the Golden Era of bodybuilding typically sported smaller waists and an[**incredible V-taper**](https://generationiron.com/golden-era-workout/) as a result. But what actually is the V-taper and how can you effectively attain it?

Many people will say that the V-taper is pure [**genetics**](https://generationiron.com/truth-genetics-bodybuilding/), and while that does reign true to an extent, how you diet and train has a large effect on your shape. Our team at **[Generation Iron](https://generationiron.com/)** is going to break it down.

### The V-Taper Overview

The V-Taper simply refers to the V-shape of the torso and is a look that is absolutely paramount in bodybuilding. It refers to the shape from the shoulders to waist, that forms a V. The look was popularized by Golden Era bodybuilders like [**Frank** **Zane**](https://generationiron.com/frank-zane-profile-stats/), Franco Columbu, and [**Arnold** **Schwarzenegger**](https://generationiron.com/arnold-approved-training-program/). If you look at them, they had the broad [**shoulders**](https://generationiron.com/best-shoulder-exercises/) and wide [**backs**](https://generationiron.com/best-back-exercises/) that gradually transitioned to a slim waist, forming the perfect V.

The V-Taper is more of an aesthetic look that is still popular amongst today’s bodybuilders, but you will see it more prominently amongst the **classic and men’s physique** divisions. Bodybuilders like [**Chris** **Bumstead**](https://generationiron.com/chris-bumstead-profile/#:~:text=Chris%20Jonathan%20Bumstead%20is%20a,%2C%202021%2C%20and%202022%20consecutively.) and [**Breon** **Ansley**](https://generationiron.com/breon-ansley-profile/) are prime examples of this physique.

### How to Obtain the V-Taper

The ultimate V-Taper can be attained through appropriate strength training and nutrition. Due to the shape and size of the muscles, the V-taper is actually a very natural look. However, carrying excess [**body** **fat**](https://generationiron.com/best-fat-burners-men/), poor-quality strength training, and [**postural** **issues**](https://generationiron.com/posterior-chain-training/) can all interfere with it.

An individual with a well developed V-taper will have large shoulders and a wide back to form the upper portion of the V. They will also have a low body fat percentage and narrow waist to form the lower portion of the V. Remember, you probably can’t have a V-Taper if you have a muffin top! A slim waist gives the illusion of an even wider back, and a wider back gives the illusion of even a smaller waist.

So how can you make it happen?

![v taper](https://generationiron.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/wesley-vissers-bodybuilder-y-taper-header-1.jpg)Image credit: Wesley Vissers

### Key V-Taper Musculature

It is important to realize that a number of training principles are applied and specific muscles are developed in order to maintain a well-rounded physique. In order to develop the V-taper most effectively, there are three main muscle groups that should be prioritized as significant growth in these areas will have the most impact on creating the V-taper.

#### 1) Latissimus Dorsi

The latissimus dorsi, lats for short, are big wing-like muscles that originate from the lower back and stretch all the way up to the shoulders. The lats are one of the biggest components of the V-taper, as building size in them will increase back width and contribute most significantly towards developing the V-shape that so many desire.

Additionally increasing back size and width will also help give the illusion of a smaller waist and enhance overall aesthetics. They contract to control a range of shoulder movements and also provide stability to the spine.

Vertical and horizontal pulling movements such as [**deadlifts**](https://generationiron.com/performing-perfect-barbell-deadlift/), [**lat** **pulldowns**](https://generationiron.com/lat-pulldown-guide/), rows, and [**pull**– **ups**](https://generationiron.com/pullup-variations-workout/) are all highly effective lat exercises for both thickness and width.

#### 2) Deltoids

The deltoids, delts for short, are the muscles of the shoulders. While the delts are looked at as just one muscle, they actually have three individual portions – **anterior, middle and posterior.**Building large shoulders is an essential component of the V-taper as this will substantially add width to the top portion of the V.

As with the lats, the delts contract in order to drive a range of shoulder movements while simultaneously stabilizing the joint.

To effectively work the delts the focus should be on all three angles of the deltoid.

#### 3) Abdominals

The muscle group that is most associated with the V-taper is the [**abdominals**](https://generationiron.com/best-ab-exercises/), abs for short. When it comes to aesthetics, well-defined abdominals tend to be the most eye-catching element of all.

![abs](https://generationiron.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/2-2.jpg)

While it is the reduction of body fat that will contribute the most to abdominal aesthetics, specific ab-targeted exercises can increase the definition of the muscles. If the abs aren’t defined, low body fat won’t matter too much.

The abs are often misunderstood as just one muscle; in fact, there are four individual abdominal muscles – the rectus abdominis, transverse abdominis, external oblique, and internal oblique.

These muscles work to control and stabilize the many movements of the trunk. There are a number of effective ab-targeted exercises including sit-ups, crunches, and isometric holds.

### Training Principles for V-Taper Development

There are a number of principles that must be applied to your training in order to bring about efficient changes in muscle size and contribute towards V-taper development.

#### 1) Form Over Weight

While training volume is undoubtedly important for onsetting muscle growth, it is futile if each exercise is performed poorly. Form means everything, and what you get out of each rep means even more. Heavy weight means nothing if your [**form is sloppy**](https://generationiron.com/truths-exercise-form/) or the reps are too quick.

For optimal muscle growth, time under tension should be prioritized ( [1](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3285070/)). As the name suggests, this is simply the time that the muscle spends experiencing stress. Poor exercise execution or rushing through each rep will not maximize [**time under tension**](https://generationiron.com/time-under-tension-growth/) and the rate of muscle growth may be affected as a consequence.

If you are a beginner, start light and focus on the movements required and muscles being worked. Be willing to spend time and effort on mastering the exercise and establishing a “[**mind-muscle connection**](https://generationiron.com/improve-your-mind-muscle-connection/)”. To effectively do this, look to slow down the eccentric (lowering) phase of the movement of the exercise and focus on squeezing the working muscle with each and every rep.

Once you have mastered the movements required for the exercise, then you can begin to add significant load. Using this approach will not only allow you to add strength and size most efficiently, it will also promote exercise safely and lower the risk of sustaining an injury.

#### 2) High Volume, Heavy Loads

When it comes to optimizing muscle growth, the research is clear – [**volume is king**](https://generationiron.com/upper-threshold-for-volume/). High volume has been associated with the greatest improvements in muscle mass ( [2](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/30153194)).

As a result, the following V-taper program uses advanced training methods such as supersets, tri-sets, and giant sets in order to substantially increase the volume.

Furthermore, the rep range has been kept low to moderate to allow for the use of heavy weight. Using heavy loads will create a large degree of mechanical tension and muscle damage – both of which are key components of muscle hypertrophy (growth).

The traditional belief is that the 6-12 range is optimal for muscle growth ( [3](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28834797)). However, while the following program does primarily utilize heavy loads and low-moderate reps, there are also a number of accessory exercises that require a high number of reps to be completed.

#### 3) Mechanical Tension

As touched on in the previous section, mechanical tension is one of the biggest driving factors in bringing about significant muscle growth ( [4](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5495834/)).

Mechanical tension refers to the amount of stress that is applied to the muscle. The more tension applied to the muscle, the greater the adaptation will be. While mechanical tension is most commonly associated with heavy, compound lifts, there are a number of other methods that will also effectively apply mechanical tension.

The program uses a variety of exercises and training techniques such as partial reps, isometrics, and eccentrics, in an attempt to manipulate mechanical tension and hypertrophy.

#### 4) Muscle Pump

In bodybuilder training, [“ **the pump**“](https://generationiron.com/mutant-pump-review/) is something that sought after in the majority of training sessions. The muscle pump occurs when blood rushes to the working muscle causing it to swell and increase in size. As a result, the working muscles will feel full, tight and fatigued.

Muscle pump is typically achieved by performing high repetitions while maintaining tension on the muscle at all times, and many use the pump as a method of encouraging muscle growth. It is theorized that the rush of nutrient-rich blood to the muscle may assist in the repair process and enhance growth.

In the program, the giant set will undoubtedly contribute towards a pump, however, there are specific “finisher” exercises incorporated into the sessions that have been designed to specifically bring about the pump.

![](https://generationiron.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/04/artemus-dolgin-tapered-waist-header.jpg)

### V-Taper Training Program

This section will provide detail on a training program that will have a substantial impact on V-Taper development.

As demonstrated below, the program used is from our own Generation Iron team member, who also competes in the NPC, [**Dylan Wolfinger**](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dylan-wolfinger/). These are the exercises he utilizes to build his V-Taper in order to put on size during the offseason.

![](https://generationiron.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/vtaper.jpg)Image courtesy of Dylan Wolfinger

#### **For The Lats**

- **Lat pulldowns:** 4 sets of 12 reps
- **Dumbbell row:** 4 sets of 10 reps
- **Cable pullover:** 4 sets of 15 reps
- **Barbell row:** 4 sets of 8 reps
- **Pull-ups:** 4 sets till failure

#### **For the Deltoids**

- **Dumbbell shoulder press:** 5 sets of 8 reps
- **Dumbbell rear delt fly:** 5 sets of 10 reps
- **Dumbbell lateral raises:** 4 sets of 12 reps
- **Cable face pulls:** 4 sets of 12 reps
- **Cable lateral raise:** 4 sets until failure

#### **For the Abdominals**

Dylan trains abdominals every other day and alternates between a few exercises, picking one exercise per day and rolling with it.

- **Russian twists super setted with lying leg raises:** 4 sets of 12-15
- **Hanging leg raises:** 4 sets of 15
- **Cable crunches:** 4 sets of 15

Considering that volume plays such a huge role in muscular hypertrophy, having such a large volume of upper body training will optimally accelerate muscle growth. As you can see, Dylan uses high volume in his training. However, it is important to control each rep and really squeezing the muscle, ensuring you get the most out of what you put in.

### Do Legs Contribute to the V-Taper?

While developing the size of the upper body will have the most telling impact on the V-taper, [**training**](https://generationiron.com/2023-diet-training-transformation-program/) the lower body can also positively impact V-taper development. Bigger [**legs**](https://generationiron.com/best-leg-exercises/) also give the illusion of a smaller waist, the wider your lats look. Your V-taper will be like that of the Golden Era bodybuilders.

In addition, it is important that you do not neglect training the lower body and maintain leg size and strength.

Below is a quick leg workout Dylan also uses when trying to put on offseason mass.

#### **For the Legs**

- **Squats:** 4 sets of 10 reps
- **Hack squat:** 4 sets of 12 reps
- **Lying hamstring curl:** 4 sets of 12 reps
- **Leg extensions:** 4 sets of 12 reps

### Diet for V-Taper Development

When it comes to changes in body composition, there is no doubt that strength training is important and can bring about significant changes. However, nutrition also plays a highly crucial role in altering your body composition, and it unfortunately is something often overlooked.

As mentioned, carrying excess body fat will interfere with creating the V-taper. It is necessary to lean down to the point where muscles become more visible. If body fat percentage is too high, these muscles will be hidden under a layer of body fat thus impact overall aesthetics.

To facilitate substantial changes in body fat, look to restrict your calorie intake on non-training days to place you in a deficit. During training days, consume for maintenance. This does not mean deplete yourself completely, but maybe find some ways that work for you to drop fat and keep the waist slim while being able to maintain or even put on muscle mass.

Perhaps focus your [**diet**](https://generationiron.com/fitness-model-diet/) more on [**protein**](https://generationiron.com/best-protein-powders/) intake rather than [**carbohydrates**](https://generationiron.com/carbohydrates-muscles/). There are a multitude of different ways to achieve the look you want, but every single person is different. While we can give suggestions, it is up to you to find a diet that works for you!

We have plenty of diet guides on our site to help you succeed in hitting your goals!

### Supplementation for the V Taper

As stated before, the V-taper is a mix of low body fat and good muscle definition. Diet and training can get you to that point, but supplementation is something that can possibly accelerate that process.

Supplementation can be just as important in obtaining your goals as diet and training. Supplements are meant to give you the nutrients that you need, but may not get enough of with just diet. They can help you obtain better pumps, more muscle growth, accelerated fat loss, and much more. What are some basic supplements that could help you hit your goals?

- **Fat Burners**
- **Protein Powders**
- **[Multivitamins](https://generationiron.com/best-multivitamins-bodybuilding/)**
- **[Pre-workout](https://generationiron.com/best-pre-workout-supplements/)**
- **[Creatine](https://generationiron.com/best-creatine-supplements/)**

These are just a few [**supplements**](https://generationiron.com/best-bodybuilding-supplements/) to help you get to your goals. There are plenty more you can look into!

### Testosterone

When it comes to building a v-taper, having adequate testosterone is crucial. Now your supplement and diet routine helps with this, but it may not be enough, which is where testosterone replacement therapy comes into play.

**[Titan Medical](https://titanmedicalcenter.com/) is a trusted option for men seeking effective and reliable testosterone replacement therapy (TRT).** They specialize in personalized treatment plans designed to restore healthy hormone levels, improve energy, enhance muscle growth, and support overall well-being. With a focus on patient care, Titan Medical provides expert guidance, ongoing monitoring, and proven solutions to ensure safe and optimized results. For those experiencing low testosterone symptoms, Titan Medical offers a professional and dependable path toward better health and performance.

### Final Word

The V-Taper is something that can really give you a more aesthetic look. The broad shoulders that tie into the wide lats, and come down to a small waist really make you look like a Golden Era bodybuilder.

By following the V-taper program and applying the recommended training, nutrition, and supplementation principles, you will bring effectively bring about substantial changes to body composition and inevitably achieve the V-taper.

web.markdown:
[Skip to Content](https://www.menshealth.com/fitness/a69179659/v-taper-workout-plan/#main-content)

![workout. gym. weight training. bodybuilding. bodybuilder having training in the gym](https://hips.hearstapps.com/hmg-prod/images/workout-gym-weight-training-bodybuilding-royalty-free-image-1761834137.pjpeg?crop=0.669xw:1.00xh;0.109xw,0&resize=640:*)

milanvirijevic//Getty Images

![Estimated read time](https://www.menshealth.com/_assets/design-tokens/fre/static/icons/clock-regular.4ddebeb.svg)5 min read

**WHEN YOU TAKE** off your shirt, the characteristics of a fit, athletic body are hard to miss. Whether you’re bare-chested at the beach, peeling off clothes in the bedroom, or playing for the skins side in pickup basketball, having washboard abs, lobster-claw triceps, and sculpted pecs certifies that your diet and training regimens are on point.

But there’s only one hardbody hallmark that can’t be disguised easily by clothing: a V-taper. The inverted-triangle upper body musculature is what separates the less focused gym rats from true bodybuilders—both because of the dedication and discipline required to achieve the look, and the smart, functional reasoning behind it.

## What Is a V-Taper?

When you achieve a V-taper, your torso resembles the 22nd letter of the English alphabet with broad shoulders and a lean, narrow waist. If you’re a man of math, your goal is a shoulder circumference that’s about 1.6 times greater than that of your hips—the same “golden ratio” that’s found in nearly every perfectly proportioned structure on the planet from the face of Mona Lisa to the Great Pyramid of Giza.

It's also a sign that your workout plan is balanced, since you're putting as much (or more) attention into training your back as you are the [mirror muscles](https://www.menshealth.com/fitness/a36789933/anterior-chain/) on your front, a common mistake among less-informed gym aesthetes. That last point is more important than you might think. In addition to just looking good, a V-taper can boost your performance both in the gym and beyond.

That’s because the muscles of your back are not only the source of your pulling power, but are also key players in your body’s “ [posterior chain](https://www.menshealth.com/fitness/a35383244/posterior-chain/),” the string of muscles that run from your traps to your calves that’s largely responsible for generating athletic power.

"The V-Taper is more about the back muscle than anything (which is also why there's underrated correlation to it very often being "healthy" for you long-term)," says _Men's Health_ fitness director [Ebenezer Samuel, C.S.C.S.](https://www.instagram.com/ebenezersamuel23/) "To build the V-Taper, you need to build lats and rear delts, both of which improve posture, help alleviate back pain, and add protective muscle around your shoulders for shoulder health."

## How to Build a V-Taper Physique

Achieving a V-taper hinges on maximizing the size and strength of your back and shoulder muscles, including your traps, rhomboids, and obliques, but especially your deltoids and lats. Like the rest of your posterior chain, these muscles are given short shrift in most guys’ workouts, so bringing them more into the spotlight typically produces gains quickly. Focus on sticking to an active routine that will help you to stay lean along with your goal to build muscle; depending on your starting point, [body recomposition](https://www.menshealth.com/fitness/a63227245/body-recomposition/) might be a good approach to achieving a V taper.

But don't be too focused on pure width to expand the top of that V shape. "A misunderstood bit about the V-taper is \[people assume\] you want to do wide-grip pullups and pulldowns," Samuel notes. "Truth is, these aren't the best option for really building lat width, because they don't let you optimally stretch the target tissues. So don't go overly wide on either idea."

If you need some inspiration, try these workout routines, which are designed specifically to give the top of your posterior chain the type of architecture that will make you a perfect fit for athletic-cut shirts.

## The V-Taper Row Series

![Play video](data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="%23ffffff" viewBox="0 0 256 256">%0A%09<path d="M240,128a15.74,15.74,0,0,1-7.6,13.51L88.32,229.65a16,16,0,0,1-16.2.3A15.86,15.86,0,0,1,64,216.13V39.87a15.86,15.86,0,0,1,8.12-13.82,16,16,0,0,1,16.2.3L232.4,114.49A15.74,15.74,0,0,1,240,128Z">%0A</path></svg>)![Video poster](https://hips.hearstapps.com/vidthumb/23df528b-6b78-4e16-87de-cfc84beda8d6/thumb_1920x1080_00001_1550594498_20260.jpg?crop=1xw:1xh;center,top)

You're hitting a horizontal pull concept, which will be much more shoulder safe for most guys than vertical pulling (think pullups or lat pulldowns). As you alternate between flared row reps and standard reps, you'll shift the focus from the rear delts and rhomboids (flared) and lats (standard)—both key muscles to developing that V shape.

**How to Do It:**

- Adjust the bench so that you can hinge at the hips and lean one arm on the headrest.
- Hold a dumbbell in the other hand. Your feet should be squared, with your knees slightly bent. Squeeze your core to keep your spine straight as you lean over the bench.
- Perform 2 row reps, flaring your elbow out. To do this, the hand holding the weight should be facing behind you.
- After the flared reps, complete 1 standard row rep with your elbow close to your body and your hand facing inward. Pause at the top of the rep for 1 second and squeeze your shoulders together before lowering to the starting position.
- Repeat for 3 to 4 sets of 3 to 4 clusters of reps

## The 5-Minute V-Taper Workout

![Play video](data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="%23ffffff" viewBox="0 0 256 256">%0A%09<path d="M240,128a15.74,15.74,0,0,1-7.6,13.51L88.32,229.65a16,16,0,0,1-16.2.3A15.86,15.86,0,0,1,64,216.13V39.87a15.86,15.86,0,0,1,8.12-13.82,16,16,0,0,1,16.2.3L232.4,114.49A15.74,15.74,0,0,1,240,128Z">%0A</path></svg>)![Video poster](https://hips.hearstapps.com/vidthumb/76035746-e08d-4de7-8ca9-e91f99a1c7a2/thumb_1920x1080_00002_1711722793_1945.jpg?crop=1xw:1xh;center,top)

This quick-hitting 20-minute session is more comprehensive, bringing the anterior muscles (shoulders, chest, and core) into the mix with press and pullover movements to go along with a row concept. You'll move fast, making this a go-to routine when you're low on time but still need to chase your goals.

**Directions:** _Perform each exercise for 30 seconds, resting for 10 seconds before the next movement. Repeat for 3 total rounds._

## Eccentric-Focused Push Press

**How to Do It:**

- Stand holding a pair of dumbbells in a neutral grip (palms facing one another) at shoulder height in a rack position. Squeeze your shoulder blades, abs, and glutes to create tension.
- Dip slightly by bending your knees, maintaining core tension, then drive through your heels and extend your hips to stand straight up and punch the weights overhead, using the momentum created to help power the movement.
- Lower the weights back down slowly, using a three-count to descend back to the starting position.

## Wide-Grip Bent-Over Row

**How to Do It:**

- Stand holding a pair of dumbbells in a neutral grip. Push your butt back, then hinge at the waist to lower your torso into a bent-over position, keeping your arms straight with the weights in front of your body.
- Row the weights by raising your elbows up and out away from your body. Squeeze your upper back and pause for a brief count at the top of the movement.
- Reverse the movement, lowering the weight back to the starting position under control.

## Hollow Hold Dumbbell Pullover

**How to Do It:**

- Get into a hollow hold position on the floor holding one dumbbell with both hands by one end. Your lower back should be planted on the floor, with your legs extended and your feet and upper back slightly elevated. Squeeze your abs and glutes to maintain tension.
- Extend your arms up above your chest. Keeping your arms straight and bracing your core, reach back behind your shoulders as far as you can while maintaining your position.
- Move your arms back to the starting position, fighting to keep your hollow hold posture.
